marketState: neutral, mildly bearish near-term tilt inside a balanced range
summary: Seasonality data (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) is entirely missing in this snapshot, so the highest-priority long-term signal cannot be confirmed and conviction is capped. Weather demand is running hot vs the 5-year normal (+12.6% 1-7D, +11.1% 8-16D, both in the upper half of range) but cooled -9.4 over the last 24h, a supportive-but-fading driver. Price is sitting almost exactly on modeled fair value now ($2.940 vs $2.917 fair, +$0.023 spread, status FAIR), while the fair-value curve itself drifts up to $3.038 by plus21D, so there is no clean cheap/rich edge today. Technicals are broadly bearish (5 of 6 timeframes trending down, 50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe) even though 4h/1d RSI (24.9/35.6) and a sub-lower-Bollinger daily close flag oversold conditions that argue against chasing shorts. Storage is a genuine bearish anchor (+184 Bcf vs 5y, this week's build beat forecast by 72 Bcf) while COT managed-money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, i.e. not an extreme, one-sided position either way.

longTerm.rationale: The top-priority long-term inputs (seasonality vsMedian, probHigher_1_2_3_4w, typicalMove4w, reliability) are all null, so there is no seasonal edge to lean on. Remaining drivers are mixed: bullish 8-16D weather demand (+11.1% vs 5y) and a fair-value curve that rises toward $3.04 by plus21D argue for upside, but a +184 Bcf storage surplus vs 5y and a bearish build surprise argue for a supply cushion that caps rallies.
longTerm.invalidation: A confirmed seasonal bias (once seasonality data populates) pointing opposite to any position taken, or storage deviation vs 5y flipping from surplus to deficit, would change this read.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality metrics to populate and for price to break decisively outside the plus21D fair-value band ($2.538-$3.538), or for the storage surplus vs 5y to narrow meaningfully, before committing directionally.
